content_markdown: A gas is taken through a cyclic process $A \rightarrow B \rightarrow C \rightarrow A$ as shown in the $P$-$V$ diagram. The work done by the gas during one complete cycle is

diagram_metadata.description: A P-V diagram showing a triangular cycle. Point A is at (6.0, 2.0), Point B is at (10.0, 8.0), and Point C is at (10.0, 2.0). The cycle goes clockwise from A to B, then vertically down to C, then horizontally back to A. Pressure P is in kPa and Volume V is in m³.

vibe_explanation: The work done in a cyclic process is equal to the area enclosed by the cycle on a P-V diagram. The shape is a right-angled triangle with a base $\Delta V = (10.0 - 6.0) = 4.0\text{ m}^3$ and a height $\Delta P = (8.0 - 2.0) = 6.0\text{ kPa}$. The area is $\frac{1}{2} \times \text{base} \times \text{height} = \frac{1}{2} \times 4.0\text{ m}^3 \times 6.0\text{ kPa} = 12.0\text{ kJ}$. Since the cycle is clockwise, the net work done by the gas is positive.
